inline_comments.message:
May we call this external-schema-manager?
Just to show, it is about external schemas, which are not managed in ONAP?

I think, 1.3.6 is the current version, right?
With this update (which is a new functionality), we shall probably updated to the next minor version (1.4.0).
pass validatorCache instance via constructor. Do not create instance in constructor.

move this line to try section.

group 49 and 52 line in single method: resolveValidator
create single return point in method.

extract to const with prefix: DEFAULT_

here and below
if class has private constructor it must be marked as final
if you return collection outside the object pls remember to make copy or unmodifiable collection

it is better to design code by using value object, not util class.
If you create class Url(String url) with dedicated methods such as getUrlPart(), getFragmetPart(), etc. then code will be more object oriented.
extract to const and give proper name for 0. This is magic word code smell.

This file is waiting for merge of https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/dcaegen2/services/sdk/+/110605.

When it will be merged, we will rebase this MR and this file will have no changes.

It is being done over there: https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/dcaegen2/services/sdk/+/110605.

When it will be merged, we will rebase this MR so 1.4.0 version is set.
I think this methods may be not static and fileName may be passed in constructor as class parameter

I think, that this class and classes JsonFragmentRetriever, JsonNodeConverter may be merged in to one class responsible for JsonNode creation, especially that this method uses method from JsonNodeConverter
I think this methods may be not static and schemaReference may be passed in constructor
Due to fact there is quite a lot of logic responsible for instantiating UrlMapper, I think it would be reasonable to move it to Factory or Supplier
I think ObjectMapper can be instantiate once and then used for both readTree method.
I think this method invocation should be in previous line in //when and the outcome should be placed here, it would be more readable

I merged SchemaReferenceJsongenerator and JsonNodeConverter, as one is used by the other and they are small. JsonFragmentRetriever imo should stay as is becouse it has functionality not related to the other two classes and is used in diferrent place.
ONAP style guide says LOGGER should NOT be capitalized
ONAP style guide says LOGGER should NOT be capitalized
ONAP coding standards say that LOGGER should NOT be capitalized
ONAP coding standards say that LOGGER should NOT be capitalized

Consider to replace if-else with the "?:" Java operator

return !isAbsoulute(internalReference) ? ROOT_REFERENCE + internalReference : internalReference;

I know its ok from point of Java conventions however in sdk I can see that in such cases we keep full stops between words.
